title: clianthus formosus
intro: Clianthus formosus, also known as the New Zealand kowhai or parrot bush, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including as a treatment for fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Clianthus formosus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
